# Vertex AI Release Notes

## Latest Updates (March 2026)

**March 16, 2026**: Vertex Explainable AI has been deprecated.

**March 12, 2026**: The Gen AI evaluation service now supports "evaluating partner models, such as Anthropic and Llama models."

**March 5, 2026**: Vector Search 2.0 reached general availability, introducing collections, auto-embeddings, hybrid search capabilities, and enhanced security features.

**March 3, 2026**: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite became available in public preview as a cost-efficient model optimized for low-latency use cases.

## Major Features & Services

**Generative AI Models**:
- Gemini 3.1 Flash, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite
- Veo 3.1 video generation with extended features
- Partner models: Claude Opus/Sonnet (Anthropic), Llama variants, Mistral offerings
- Open models: Qwen, DeepSeek, Llama Guard

**Vertex AI Agent Engine**:
Sessions, Memory Bank, and Code Execution moved from preview to GA. Features include bidirectional streaming, A2A protocol support, and enterprise security (CMEK, PSC-I, HIPAA compliance).

**Vertex AI Workbench**:
M139 release includes JupyterLab 4+ support, managed credentials, Gemini CLI integration, and Confidential Computing support.

**Vector Search 2.0**:
Provides collections unifying data/vectors, auto-embeddings, hybrid search combining vector/full-text/semantic ranking.

## Deprecations & Removals

- Vertex Explainable AI (March 2026)
- Feature Store Optimized online serving (February 2026)
- Multiple video generation preview endpoints (deadline: April 2, 2026)
- Image generation preview endpoints (deadline: March 19, 2026)
- Gemini 1.5 models unavailable for new projects (April 2025)

## Key Pricing Changes

Vertex AI Agent Engine runtime pricing was lowered; Sessions, Memory Bank, and Code Execution began charging February 11, 2026. Regional pricing expansion across Asia-Pacific, Europe, and Americas regions.
